english: in the afternoons (about noon till 2 p.m.)
notes: Does not mean "this afternoon". The tone on "ē" does not change.

english: measure word for affairs / matters / things
rest (noun and verb) / take a break / time off / be absent
(interrogative sound at end of clause/sentence; expects an affirmative answer)
notes: A common affirmative sound answering "hoⁿh" is "hèⁿ".
Lit., "sun-time". "jit" does not change tone.

english: to arrive / reach (destination); up to (and including)

notes: At end of clause/sentence (but not suffixed to a verb), "khit" does not change tone and "khì" is enclitic. As suffix to a verb at end of clause/sentence, both become Double Enclitics.

english: to come up; to get out of bed
notes: At end of clause/sentence (but not suffixed to a verb), "khit" does not change tone and "lâi" is enclitic. As suffix to a verb at end of clause/sentence, both become Double Enclitics.

english: to lie at rest; to sleep
notes: khì khùn = to go to bed (distinguish from "to go to sleep" — No. 27).
Distinguish from "to go to bed". Tones on "khùn" and "khí" never change.

english: (exclamation affirming or emphasizing the word(s) it accompanies)

loh-chhia = to alight from a vehicle. At end of clause/sentence "loh" does not change tone; "lâi/khì" are enclitics.
